text: The surgeon mobilizes the liver from the diaphragm, revealing the supra-hepatic inferior vena cava (IVC). Using firefly mode, the ICG-stained liver metastasis is identified. Adhesions between the liver and the right and transverse colon are carefully mobilized due to prior surgery.
The surgeon applies a Pringle maneuver around the hepatoduodenal ligament to control blood flow, while an intraoperative ultrasound is used to screen the liver for additional metastases.

The surgeon marks the resection margin on the liver surface by incising the liver capsule. The right hepatic artery is identified, clipped, and cut. Subsequently, the right hepatic branch of the portal vein is looped with a suture, clipped, and cut.
The surgeon mobilizes the liver from the inferior vena cava (IVC), selectively clipping and cutting the Spigellian veins.

The liver parenchymal dissection is performed using the three-device technique, involving a laparoscopic aquachet, bipolar Maryland forceps, and monopolar scissors. The right bile duct is identified intrahepatically, clipped, and cut during the robotic-assisted procedure.
The surgeon identifies and divides the right hepatic vein using a stapler. The liver is then packed into a retrieval bag and extracted through the scar from a previous cholecystectomy.
